methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The total entitlement of any distributable ERC20 across all token supply must never exceed the contract's actual balance of that ERC20.
invariants.attack_scenario: If entitlement math rounds up or supply increases during an active distribution, the contract would attempt to distribute more rewards than it holds. This causes ERC20 transfers to revert, permanently locking the distribution process and freezing all token transfers.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall i in distributableERC20s: erc20EntitlementPerUnit[i] * totalSupply() <= IERC20(distributableERC20s[i]).balanceOf(address(this))
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_entitlement_solvency() public view { if (LockedForDistribution) { for (uint i=0; i<distributableERC20s.length; i++) { assert(erc20EntitlementPerUnit[i] * totalSupply() <= IERC20(distributableERC20s[i]).balanceOf(address(this))); } } }

invariants.description: Every address tracked in the `holders` array must have a strictly positive token balance.
invariants.attack_scenario: If zero-balance addresses (e.g., address(0) during burns, or recipients of 0-amount transfers) are added or left in the holders array, the array will grow unboundedly. This leads to an Out-Of-Gas DoS during `distributeToAllHolders`, permanently freezing the protocol's yield distribution.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_holders_balance() public view { for(uint i=0; i<holders.length; i++) { assert(balanceOf(holders[i]) > 0); } }

invariants.description: The sum of balances of all addresses in the `holders` array must exactly equal the total supply of the token.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the holders array does not accurately reflect all token holders (e.g., a holder is missing), those users will not receive their proportional revenue distributions, leading to yield theft and trapped rewards in the contract.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_holders_sum_equals_supply() public view { uint256 sum = 0; for(uint i=0; i<holders.length; i++) { sum += balanceOf(holders[i]); } assert(sum == totalSupply()); }

invariants.description: The total supply of the ERC20 token must remain strictly constant while a distribution is in progress.
invariants.attack_scenario: If tokens can be minted or burned during an active distribution phase, the entitlement per unit calculated at the start becomes invalid. This allows attackers to double-dip rewards or causes the distribution to fail due to insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_locked_supply() public view { if (LockedForDistribution) { assert(totalSupply() == supplyAtDistributionStart); } }

invariants.description: The protocol must be the owner of all LiquidInfrastructureNFTs listed in the `ManagedNFTs` array.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an NFT is released or transferred but remains in the ManagedNFTs array, calls to `withdrawFromManagedNFTs` will revert when attempting to withdraw from an unowned NFT, causing a DoS on revenue collection for all holders.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_managed_nft_ownership() public view { for(uint i=0; i<ManagedNFTs.length; i++) { assert(LiquidInfrastructureNFT(ManagedNFTs[i]).ownerOf(1) == address(this)); } }
